Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed verbal clause with infinitive construction
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To push a situation beyond acceptable limits until consequences arise
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, family conflicts, workplace tension
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional rural metaphor linked to rope labor and agricultural tools
First Example Sentence: El cap l'ha tirà tròpp la cuerda e incoeu tücc hinn staa contra de lü.
Second Example Sentence: Se te continüet inscì, finirét per tirà tròpp la cuerda con i amis.
Third Example Sentence: La discussiun l'è degenerada quand quajedün l'ha tirà tròpp la cuerda.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Comparative adjectival idiom with existential verb
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To behave in a bizarre or eccentric way
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Urban Milanese idiom comparing unstable behavior to a protruding balcony

Second Example Sentence: Dopo tri cafè l'era completament föra come on balcon.
Third Example Sentence: I disen che l'artista l'è semper staa föra come on balcon.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To suffer deprivation or endure hardship silently

First Example Sentence: Durante la guèra tanta gent la mangiava la foeuja per campà.
Second Example Sentence: El non el cuntava semper de quand hinn staa costrett a mangià la foeuja.
Third Example Sentence: Quella famiglia l'ha mangià la foeuja prima de trovà fortuna.

Idiom (Lombard): Mettegh el car davanti ai bò
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Infinitive verbal phrase with prepositional complement
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To do things in the wrong order
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Agricultural proverb involving ox-driven carts in Lombard countryside
First Example Sentence: Te stet mettend el car davanti ai bò con quel progett.
Second Example Sentence: Prima impara la teoria e pö evita de mettegh el car davanti ai bò.
Third Example Sentence: L'han comprà i mobili prima de trovà la cà: propri el car davanti ai bò.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To speak without being listened to
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to imagery of speaking to stone walls in old Lombard homes

Second Example Sentence: La maestra la diseva che spiegà a cert fioeu l'era parlà al mür.
Third Example Sentence: Te podet anca urlà, ma incoeu l'è parlà al mür.

Second Example Sentence: Per fà volontariato bisogna avègh el coeur in man.
Third Example Sentence: Anca se l'è pover, el gh'ha el coeur in man.

Idiom (Lombard): Fà de tutt on erba on fas
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed proverb with collective noun phrase
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Ancient agrarian image of bundling all herbs together regardless of type

Second Example Sentence: El giornalista l'ha sbaglià a fà de tutt on erba on fas.
Third Example Sentence: Cert discors populist hinn basaa sul fà de tutt on erba on fas.

Idiom (Lombard): Vess cont i pee per tera
